title: Thelypteris suprastrigosa
taxonomy: Thelypteris suprastrigosa is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of fern. More specifically, it belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as the marsh ferns. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other species within the Thelypteridaceae family. Thelypteris suprastrigosa is also classified as a member of the genus Thelypteris, which consists of approximately 180 species of ferns. The classification of Thelypteris suprastrigosa is further supported by molecular studies and genetic analysis.

intro: Thelypteris suprastrigosa, also known as the "Supra-Strigose Lady Fern," is a species of fern native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris suprastrigosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
